title: Breaking the code: Multi-stage ‘code of conduct’ phishing campaign leads to AiTM token compromise
description:
Microsoft Defender Research observed a large-scale credential theft campaign that exemplifies this trend, using code of conduct-themed lures, a multi-step attack chain, and legitimate email services to distribute fully authenticated messages from attacker-controlled domains.

The post Breaking the code: Multi-stage ‘code of conduct’ phishing campaign leads to AiTM token compromise appeared first on Microsoft Security Blog.

title: CVE-2026-31431: Copy Fail vulnerability enables Linux root privilege escalation across cloud environments
description:
A high-severity Linux vulnerability, “Copy Fail” (CVE-2026-31431), enables root privilege escalation across cloud environments and Kubernetes workloads. With a working exploit already in the wild, organizations should act quickly to detect, mitigate, and reduce risk.

The post CVE-2026-31431: Copy Fail vulnerability enables Linux root privilege escalation across cloud environments appeared first on Microsoft Security Blog.

title: Microsoft Agent 365, now generally available, expands capabilities and integrations
description:
​Today we’re announcing the general availability of Agent 365, plus previews of new capabilities to discover and manage shadow AI agents, including local agents like OpenClaw and Claude Code.

The post Microsoft Agent 365, now generally available, expands capabilities and integrations appeared first on Microsoft Security Blog.

title: What’s new, updated, or recently released in Microsoft Security
description:
Stay ahead of emerging threats with Microsoft’s newest security innovations and updates, delivered through the In the Loop series.

The post What’s new, updated, or recently released in Microsoft Security appeared first on Microsoft Security Blog.

title: Email threat landscape: Q1 2026 trends and insights
description:
In early 2026, email threats increased with a rise in credential phishing, QR code phishing, and CAPTCHA-gated campaigns, highlighted by Microsoft’s disruption of the Tycoon2FA phishing platform which led to a 15% volume decrease and shifts in threat actor tactics.

The post Email threat landscape: Q1 2026 trends and insights appeared first on Microsoft Security Blog.

description:
Learn how Microsoft Sentinel UEBA helps defenders distinguish benign AWS activity from attacker behavior by enriching raw CloudTrail logs with clear, binary behavioral signals derived from baseline user, peer, and device behavior patterns.
